title: Presidential communication: foundational
description: Test your knowledge on presidential communication!
topic_path: How does the president communicate with the public?

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a bully pulpit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an outstanding opportunity to attract public attention
an annual speech that the president vies to Congress
an informal conversation about political issues
a twenty-first century social media platform

assessment_items.question_content:
Theodore Roosevelt first used the term “bully pulpit”.

**What did Roosevelt most likely mean by the word “bully”**? 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the same thing as “great” and “wonderful”
someone who unfairly picks on another person
the same thing as “bad” or “difficult”
something that is controversial or emotional

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes the president's special ability to attract public attention and discuss political goals?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: bully pulpit
executive privilege
fireside chat
power of persuasion

assessment_items.question_content:
**
What is one way that the president’s communication tactics have changed in the last 100 years?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They have more opportunities to speak directly to the people.
They can’t reach as big of an audience.
They’re more restrained with their speech.
They are more formal with their public communication.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these goals would a president most likely use their “bully pulpit” for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to urge the public to vote for member of their political party
to convince Congress to accept their proposed bill
to select a nominee for the Supreme Court
to issue a command to the executive branch of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**
According to the Constitution, what’s the main purpose of the State of the Union address?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to inform Congress of the nation’s economic and political situation
to update the American public on how the government is doing
to give your first official speech when you become the new president
to celebrate the progress that a particular state has made

assessment_items.question_content:
**How has the State of the Union changed over time?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is now more accessible to the American public.
It used to only be addressed to the Senate.
It used to be given by the vice president.
It is now most often delivered as a letter rather than a speech.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When does the State of the Union happen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: in January or February of each year
in January the year the president takes office
in November every four years
in the summer each year

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the Constitution, who’s the main audience for the State of the Union?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the two chambers of Congress
the American public
leaders of foreign governments
high officials from the three branches of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why does the president have to give a State of the Union address? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It’s required by the Constitution.
They don’t have to; it’s just a tradition.
The American public petitions the government for it.
Major news outlets lobby the government for it.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which president is famous for using the radio to connect with ordinary citizens?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Franklin D. Roosevelt
Theodore Roosevelt
Ronald Reagan
Donald Trump

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1981, a president went on television and made a direct plea to the American people. He asked them to call their representatives and ask them to support his tax plan. 

**Which president was this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Ronald Reagan
Harry Truman
Franklin D. Roosevelt
Theodore Roosevelt

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is one way that Barack Obama changed presidential communication with the public? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He made the @POTUS twitter account.
He was the first president to give a televised State of the Union address.
He was the first president to appear on Saturday Night Live.
He popularized direct communication through fireside chats.

assessment_items.question_content:
The president and Congress disagree over a budget proposal. The president believes tax cuts will help the economy, and the majority party in Congress believes they will hurt the economy.

**How can the president use new technology to promote their  goals in this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ask the American people over social media to call their representatives
adjourn a congressional session because the two chambers cannot agree
discuss the need for lower taxes in the State of the Union address
gather evidence to prove that the president’s tax plan is the best solution

assessment_items.question_content:
**President Franklin D. Roosevelt’s fireside chats established which of the following norms in presidential communication?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: speaking to the public with a high degree of familiarity
being formal and professional in all communication
working together with Congress to craft a unified message
criticizing the opposing political party for its lack of support

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes a fireside chat?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an informal conversation
an official declaration
a collaborative negotiation
a dramatic retelling

assessment_items.question_content:
**What’s an example of a policy goal that a president might promote in a  public broadcast?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: lowering the unemployment rate
delivering the State of the Union address
appointing all members of the cabinet
gaining popularity through their public appearances

assessment_items.question_content:
The president wants Congress to pass a budget bill, but members of Congress are resisting the president’s plan. The president decides that it would be useless to work directly with the members of Congress to reach a compromise. Instead, the president makes a public announcement about the issue. They call out Congress for being unhelpful. This puts pressure on Congress to respond in a way that satisfies the president and the public.

**What is the president doing in this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: going public
issuing a pocket veto
breaking tradition
campaigning for their party

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is publicity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the amount of media attention something receives
information that’s available to everyone
information that’s not available to the public
the amount of positive excitement there is surrounding something

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does it mean to broadcast a message?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to spread it across media outlets
to withhold a part of it from view
to say it out loud
to tailor it to a certain audience
